'''Danalbeth''' was a [[Waterworld/Legends|water world]]<ref name="Gamer1" /> located in the [[Danalbeth system/Legends|Danalbeth system]], a part of the [[Teraab sector/Legends|Teraab sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was postulated by some [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] that Danalbeth was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Graygl]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]].<ref name="Gamer1" />

At some point five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Date">{{CSWECite|II|119|Iskalonian}} states that the [[Species/Legends|species]] constituting the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group were brought to [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] approximately five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Since {{GamerCite|1|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series}} establishes that the relocation of the Iskalonian species was prefaced by the cataclysms on the species' original [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]], the cataclysms must also have occurred around that [[Time/Legends|time]].</ref> a series of cataclysms occurred on several water [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including the homeworld of the Graygl. The catastrophes left the native inhabitants helpless until a [[Unidentified spacefaring benefactors|group]] of [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] benefactors arrived and helped relocate the species of the affected worlds to the<ref name="Gamer1" /> Mid Rim<ref name="EAOC Iskalon">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]]</ref> planet [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], where the latter eventually formed the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group.<ref name="Gamer1" />

At some point during the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Shi'ido]] [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Mammon Hoole|Hoole]] was studying the ruins of the Iskalonian homeworlds to determine the nature of the cataclysms that occurred there and the identity of the Iskalonian benefactors.<ref name="Gamer1" />

==Behind the scenes==
Danalbeth was first mentioned in the article "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]," which was authored by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Joseph Bongiorno|Joe Bongiorno]] and was published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer1" /> in [[November]] [[2000]].<ref name="GamerDate">''[[Star Wars Gamer Special Edition]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Danalbeth [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Danalbeth, in grid square P-11.<ref name="TEA" />
